attempt_summary: Normalized actual outputs from artifact files.

evidence_refs: trace: Read task context and write artifacts (trace steps 1-3)
actual_outputs.json metadata (compliance flags: hard_threshold_escalation_applied=true, needs_review_record_count=1, no_external_measurements=true)
rubric_item_scores.failure_mode: Residual uncertainty due to lack of direct CSV content preview prevents per-record numeric re-check; if the CSV contains transcription or arithmetic errors they would not be caught by the trace-only evidence.
rubric_item_scores.improvement_suggestion: Include the full CSV content (or a small sample of rows) and explicit cell/formula references in field_inspection_plan.md (e.g., spreadsheet formulas or pseudocode lines) so reviewers can recompute priority_value per record and verify sensitivity-shift outputs exactly.
rubric_item_scores.notes: Deterministic precheck and execution trace show both required artifacts were created and the worker read the authoritative primary_formula and limits from the embedded packet. The trace explicitly records using priority_value = metric1*metric2 - 0.6*metric3 + metric4, applying a 5% sensitivity_shift, marking one record as needs_review, applying hard-threshold escalation, and producing cost estimates within the provided decision_budget_usd. Because the actual CSV content was not provided in previews, I rely on the provided trace and metadata which strongly indicate correct, reproducible application of the mandated formula, sensitivity shifts, and budget-aware prioritization. Minor uncertainty remains because I could not inspect raw CSV cell-by-cell to re-compute numeric priority_value matches for every record.
rubric_item_scores.reasoning_summary: Deterministic precheck and execution trace show both required artifacts were created and the worker read the authoritative primary_formula and limits from the embedded packet. Minor uncertainty remains because I could not inspect raw CSV cell-by-cell to re-compute numeric priority_value matches for every record.

attempt_summary: Normalized actual outputs from artifact files.

evidence_refs: Artifact preview: CSV first_rows and MD preview (artifact_content_previews)
rubric_item_scores.improvement_suggestion: Minor improvements: include explicit budget allocation table rows in the CSV (or a machine-readable budget_allocation.csv) to show exact dollars per record after proportional allocation, and add a short note documenting rounding handling so downstream automation can reproduce exact budget line items. Also consider explicitly listing the numeric limit values in the CSV header for absolute traceability (e.g., limit_rain_72h_mm=54.67).
rubric_item_scores.notes: Both required artifacts are present and parsed. Priority values for all four records exactly match the mandated formula (priority_value = rain_72h_mm * slope_deg - 0.6 * soil_saturation_pct + road_exposure_score) as recomputed from CSV preview values (R1=2023.93, R2=369.66, R3=-24.69, R4=140.58). Breach flags correspond to stated limits (rain>54.67, slope>7.2, soil>57.3). Hard-threshold escalation (hard_threshold_record_count=1) was applied by ranking all breaching records before non-breaching records while preserving numeric priority ordering within bands (final_rank ordering R1, R4, R2, R3). Sensitivity analysis for +/-5% is present with priority_plus5/priority_minus5 values and correctly identifies R2 as sensitive to a +5% adverse shift that would breach limits. The field inspection plan documents executable pseudocode/formulas, validation checks, sensitivity procedure, record-level citations, and budget allocation notes referencing decision_budget_usd=25000. No external data or hidden references were used.
